summary: ISS-1523 (curmudgeon c10 H2, persisting from c6/c7/c8/c9) resolved by EXP-258. WIN-010 detail_verdict_text claimed 'approximately common multiplicative error on both residuals at first order' without demonstration. Patch replaces the unargued first-order claim with a sub-dominance expectation plus a pre-registered post-2026-08-12 sensitivity check using a coverage-response exponent α ∈ {0.8, 1.0, 1.2}. Single substring patch on WIN-010 only; before-string verified unique (occurs once at offset 62604 in data/wins.json). Verdict (Std Model Explains) unchanged — only the strength of the 2026 nine-station discriminator's robustness statement is softened.
detail: Curmudgeon c10 H2 offered two repair options: (a) demonstrate the near-common multiplier across 30°–60° CGM with a worked example, or (b) soften to a sub-dominance claim plus a pre-registered coverage-exponent sensitivity check. EXP-258 takes option (b) with a cleaner pre-registration than the curmudgeon's literal phrasing (which had a typo 'in 2-3 the post-event analysis'). Falsifiable binary outcome: ranking stable across α ∈ {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} → linear-coverage adequate; ranking flips → conductivity-integral nonlinearity needs explicit treatment. Kernel of truth surfaced in EXP: residual numerator R_i is literally the same number in both fits (only comparison target differs), so multiplicative coverage correction contaminates both fits identically as a per-station factor m(c_i); 'approximately common' is plausibly correct in the α ≈ 1 regime but the demonstration is not given in the WIN. Pre-registration is the conservative repair.

patches_summary.summary: detail_verdict_text — replace 'approximately common multiplicative error' sentence with sub-dominance + pre-registered α ∈ {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} sensitivity check

attention_inbox_resolution.resolution: EXP-258 written for ISS-1523. Decider can apply the patch (single substring replacement, before-string verified unique) and close ISS-1523 alongside marking the inbox item resolved. ISS-1522 (Chapman-Sq hyphenation, c10 H1) and ISS-1524 (K&C page range, c10 H4 — note: this looks already addressed by EXP-244 which fixed K&C 2040-2049 cluster-wide; flagging for decider re-check) are out of scope for EXP-258.

do_not: Do NOT bundle ISS-1522 (Chapman–Sq hyphenation) into this EXP — that is a cluster-wide stylistic choice across WIN-010/025/068 and warrants a separate cluster patch.

Do NOT close ISS-1524 based on this EXP — ISS-1524 is the K&C page range issue and was nominally addressed by EXP-244's 2040-2049 correction. Decider should re-check whether ISS-1524 was opened in error or is asking for something distinct (e.g., the publisher TOC verification footnote that c10 H4 mentions).

Do NOT delete the entire residual-comparison paragraph as a 'simpler' fix — it carries the WIN's pre-registered nine-station discriminator framing, which is load-bearing for the verdict's prospective-test claim.
